2605.30333.title: COMPOSE: Composing Future Theorems from Citations and Formal Structure
2605.30333.paper_summary: A plausible future mathematical claim must satisfy two constraints: it should follow the direction of prior work and respect the formal dependencies that constrain what can validly follow. Existing approaches typically model only one of these sources, producing claims that are either weakly grounded or insufficiently motivated. We introduce grounded future mathematical generation, where the goal is to generate a plausible future theorem-like claim for an anchor paper using two complementary sources of context: its scientific citation graph and aligned formal theorem dependency graph. To address this setting, we propose COMPOSE, a dual-graph framework that conditions a language model on both scientific citation context and formal theorem structure. To support this setting, we construct a dataset of 108K paired scientific-formal graph examples from arXiv and Mathlib, together with a benchmark of 47K future papers from 2024--2025. Experiments show that COMPOSE outperforms strong baselines on retrieval to real future papers and achieves the best overall performance under LLM-judge evaluation, producing more grounded and mathematically richer outputs. These results show that future mathematical generation benefits from combining scientific context with formal structure. Project page is available at https://david-busbib.github.io/COMPOSE-page/.
2605.30333.paper_summary_zh: 一個合理的未來數學主張必須滿足兩個約束條件：它應該遵循先前工作的方向，並尊重約束有效後續內容的正式依賴關係。現有的方法通常僅建模這些來源中的一個，產生的主張要麼基礎薄弱，要麼動機不足。我們引入了有根據的未來數學生成，其目標是為一篇基準論文生成一個合理的未來定理類主張，使用兩個互補的上下文來源：其科學引用圖和對應的正式定理依賴圖。為了解決這一情境，我們提出了COMPOSE，一個雙圖框架，將語言模型的條件設置在科學引用上下文和正式定理結構之上。為了支持這一設定，我們從arXiv和Mathlib構建了一個包含108K對應科學-正式圖例的數據集，並附上了一個來自2024-2025年的47K未來論文基準。實驗表明，COMPOSE在檢索真實未來論文方面超越了強基準，並在LLM-judge評估下達到了最佳整體性能，產生了更有根據和數學上更豐富的輸出。這些結果顯示，未來數學生成受益於科學上下文與正式結構的結合。項目頁面可在 https://david-busbib.github.io/COMPOSE-page/ 獲得。
2605.30333.author: David Busbib et.al.
